title: polycarpon polycarpoides subsp. herniarioides
intro: Polycarpon polycarpoides subsp. herniarioides is a subspecies of the Polycarpon polycarpoides plant, commonly known as the "shining mouse-ear" or "small creeping spurge". This plant is a type of succulent that belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family and is native to Europe. The medicinal properties of Polycarpon polycarpoides subsp. herniarioides include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which have been found to have health benefits such as wound healing, skin protection, and relief from minor skin irritations and infections.
